purpose: Validate that the SKILL.md description triggers correctly. Each query is a realistic user message; expected = whether the cowork-metaprompt skill should fire.

evals.prompt: Here's a prompt I'm using in Cowork — can you rewrite it so the agent handles it better? 'help me clean up my downloads folder'

I have this prompt I've been using in ChatGPT for code review. I want to port it to Claude Code — what should I change?

<pastes a 30-line system prompt>
How should I phrase a Cowork request so the agent uses my Slack MCP instead of falling back to clicking around? I keep getting it to use computer use when I don't want it to.
Can you do some metaprompting on this for me — I want it tighter and I want it to actually verify the output before claiming success: 'summarize my Q3 OKR progress'
improve this prompt: 'find interesting things in my notes'
Improve this paragraph for my landing page — make it punchier and remove the passive voice.
Rewrite this email so it sounds less stiff but still professional — I'm replying to my manager about the deadline slip.
Help me build a Cowork skill that summarizes my weekly Slack DMs into a digest. Walk me through the SKILL.md and reference file structure.
Run this for me: search my Google Drive for the Q3 board deck, summarize the appendix, and save the summary as a Word doc in my selected folder.

notes: Queries 1–5 should fire cowork-metaprompt because each is explicitly about transforming or phrasing a prompt for Cowork or Claude Code.
Query 5 is intentionally borderline — 'improve this prompt' uses the generic verb but binds it to 'this prompt', which is the calibration the description is built around. Should fire.

Queries 6–8 are the most common failure mode for over-pushy descriptions: they use 'tighten / improve / rewrite' against non-prompt artifacts (code, writing, email). Negative-trigger guidance in the description should prevent these.
Query 9 is skill-authoring — explicitly excluded by the description's 'Do NOT use to author other skills' clause.

Query 10 is task-execution — the description's 'Do NOT use to execute the underlying task' clause should prevent firing here even though it mentions Cowork primitives (Drive, workspace folder, .docx).
